scores.head_floor_note: HEAD distance is 149 because the function is held by an asmfix `replace_with_asmfile` bridge with a stub C body; when the bridge is stripped + the cheat-asm injection (371 lines) is stripped, the stub builds to build_insns=2. The 149 measurement is a sandbox-strip artifact, NOT a reflection of how close pure-C could be. The candidate's 52 is the FIRST honest pure-C measurement and is below the prior session's reported 76 floor.

sessions.lever: session 2: semantic reconstruction — GPU GP0 DMA chain packet builder. 2-arg signature (arg2/arg3 unused), goto-form clamps mirroring sibling func_8007CE0C, big-packet vs small-packet branch based on `(*(u16*)arg0 & 0x3F) || ((u16)arg0->w & 0x3F)`. Levers: m2c-shape 2-arg sig, extern s32 *D_8009BF48 forward decl, address-of D_800F187C captured into local p187C (saves lui/sw pair), *(u16*)arg0 & 0x3F (matches target's lhu instead of lw), goto-based clamps (nested-if scored 87, goto form 67 then 52).

session 3 (this session): 9 additional SOTN-allowed structural variants (variable reuse, named intermediates, operand-order swap, store reordering, signature change) — all confirm sandbox 52 floor with 0 rules retired. Cumulative ~24 negative levers across two sessions.
sessions.note: No further floor reduction; the residual 52 is list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY decisions in the GPU packet store sequence.

sessions.lever: workflow round 1 (session 4): confirmed sandbox-52 baseline via direct disable=all build and tested 6 additional structural variants from the score-52 base. All confirm the 52 floor.
sessions.note: Candidate.c body is structurally end-to-end correct (151/151 insns, $t0/$t1/$s0/$s1 register match, frame 0x40 match, packet-path store address sequences match target). Residual 52 is pure list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY in the GPU packet store sequence: target splits 0x03FFFFFF constant materialization across ~36 instructions (lui $a3 at idx 49, ori $a3 at idx 85) and interleaves BF48 OT-link load chain with mode-constant stores; my build emits them adjacent. Six new SOTN-allowed C-source restructures (store-order swap, byte-pointer flag check, typed constant, struct field signedness, parenthesization, cast variants) all confirmed the 52 floor. Cumulative ~30 negative levers across 4 sessions. Lever class matches cpu_side_move_dir_4 sched.c walls.

reviewer.evidence: Not yet invoked on the candidate. The candidate is structural pure-C with no obvious cheat constructs (no register pins, no register-asm, no volatile coercion on globals, no unused locals — the (void)arg2/arg3 is the K&R-style ABI honoring of unused params, see SOTN community standard). Invoke cheat-reviewer before any Match: commit. Manual self-audit notes the candidate's `(void)arg2; (void)arg3;` declarations have semantic purpose (silence unused-param warnings on a function whose ABI takes 4 args but logic uses 2 — the calling convention is what matters for the prologue size, and this preserves it cleanly).

remaining_gap.summary: List-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY decisions in the GPU packet store sequence. Target interleaves the 0x03FFFFFF constant materialization (lui $a3 0x3FFFFFF + ori $a3 0xFFFF split by ~20 insns) with the BF48 load chain; mine emits them adjacent. Same class as cpu_side_move_dir_4 / marionation_Exec sched.c walls. NO frame coercion, NO register allocation diffs — pure scheduling.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD's single rule is `func_8007CBB0: replace_with_asmfile "asm/funcs/func_8007CBB0.s"` — an asmfile bridge over a stub body. The candidate's score-52 form is structurally and semantically correct; to commit, the bridge rule would be retired (drop the bridge → replace stub with candidate body) only if score reaches 0. The structural shape is the right base for the close; the residual is scheduling-only.

next_hypotheses: Set up permuter/cbb0/ with a CLEAN single-function offset-0 target and base.c = preprocessed display.c with candidate body stripped to single-function. Run with --stop-on-zero and PERM_GENERAL directed macros targeting scheduling permutations. Vet any saved candidate below 52 via the cheat-reviewer agent against cheats-by-any-spelling policy.

Directed permuter from a CLEAN single-function target with the candidate as base. Per [[difficult-is-not-impossible]] section on clean targets — a whole-file target.o makes the function sit at non-zero offset, drowning the real signal. Build `asm/funcs/func_8007CBB0.s` standalone + the candidate as base.c, run with --stop-on-zero. CRITICAL: invoke cheat-reviewer on any closing form found.

BB2_SCHED_DEBUG / BB2_PRIO_DEBUG instrumented cc1 dump on the candidate. Identify which pseudo's INSN_PRIORITY needs nudging for the 0x03FFFFFF materialization to defer like target's. Same instrumented cc1 from cpu_side_move_dir_4 sessions (tmp/gccdbg/cc1) applies.

C-source restructure that changes INSN_PRIORITY of the BF48 load OR the 0x03FFFFFF materialization without adding emitted bytes. Combine-foldable chain extender style (see register-alloc-pure-c session 8 — but that exact technique was forbidden 2026-06-02 for cheat-by-spelling; a different non-cheat restructure is needed).

rejected_forms.reason: Forbidden per .claude/rules/dead-vars-local-array.md. Frame already matches target at 0x40 = 64 with the natural (void)arg2/arg3 declarations; no coercion needed.
Forbidden per .claude/rules/inline-asm-policy.md (scheduling barrier cheat). Would defeat the list-scheduler reordering but is cheat-asm.
Forbidden per .claude/rules/split-read-defeats-hoist.md (2026-05-31 update). Game-state globals were not volatile in the original source; coercion to break list-scheduler order is cheat-by-spelling.

structural_evidence.build_insns_match: 151 candidate == 151 target — EXACT match. Structural decomp is end-to-end correct.
structural_evidence.register_match: arg0 in $t0, arg1 in $t1, $s0 holds 0xE3000000, $s1 holds 0xE5000000 — all match target.
structural_evidence.small_packet_stores: 6 stores in order F1858/F185C/F1864/F1860/F1868/F186C — matches target.
structural_evidence.clamp_shape: Both clamps emit `lh; nop; addu v1,v0,zero; addiu v0,v0,-1; slt; bnez` (matches target shape, including the 'save BE78 into $v1' trick).
structural_evidence.residual: 52 masked diffs = pure list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY decisions in the constant-materialization/load chain interleaving.
